016package org.consensusj.bitcoinj.signing;
017
018import org.bitcoinj.crypto.ECKey;
019import org.bitcoinj.core.Transaction;
020import org.bitcoinj.script.ScriptException;
021import org.bitcoinj.wallet.DeterministicKeyChain;
022import org.slf4j.Logger;
023import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
024
025import java.util.List;
026import java.util.Optional;
027import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture;
028import java.util.function.Supplier;
029
030/**
031 * A template interface, implementations need only implement {@link #keyForInput(TransactionInputData)}.
032 * The {@link HDKeychainSigner} implementation can sign transactions using a <b>bitcoinj</b> {@link DeterministicKeyChain}.
033 * <p>
034 * Keys and PubKeys can be searched for, but not UTXOs or amounts
035 */
036public interface BaseTransactionSigner extends TransactionSigner {
037    Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(BaseTransactionSigner.class);
038    /**
039     * Create a signed bitcoinj transaction from the signing request
040     * <p>
041     * @param request Signing request with data for all inputs and all outputs
042     * @return A signed transaction (should be treated as immutable)
043     */
044    @Override
045    default CompletableFuture<Transaction> signTransaction(SigningRequest request) {
046        // Create a new, empty (mutable) bitcoinj transaction
047        Transaction transaction = new Transaction();
048
049        // For each output in the signing request, add an output to the bitcoinj transaction
050        request.outputs().forEach(
051                output -> transaction.addOutput(output.toMutableOutput())
052        );
053
054        // For each address in the input list, add a signed input to the bitcoinj transaction
055        request.inputs().forEach(
056                input -> addSignedInput(transaction, input, () -> new RuntimeException("Unsupported transaction input"))
057        );
058
059        log.info("signed tx: {}", transaction);
060
061        // TODO: Additional Transaction validation?
062        return verify(transaction, request.inputs())
063                .map(CompletableFuture::<Transaction>failedFuture)
064                .orElse(CompletableFuture.completedFuture(transaction));
065    }
066
067    default Optional<Exception> verify(Transaction tx, List<TransactionInputData> reqInputs) {
068        return tx.getInputs().stream().flatMap(i ->
069            verifyInput(tx, i.getIndex(), reqInputs.get(i.getIndex())).stream()
070        ).findFirst();
071    }
072
073    default Optional<Exception> verifyInput(Transaction tx, int index, TransactionInputData inputData) {
074        try {
075            log.info("Verifying input {}", tx.getInputs().get(index));
076            TransactionVerification.correctlySpendsInput(tx, index, inputData.script());
077            return Optional.empty();
078        } catch (ScriptException se) {
079            log.error("Invalid input", se);
080            return Optional.of(se);
081        }
082    }
083
084    /**
085     *
086     * @param tx Mutable transaction currently being built
087     * @param in The transaction input data
088     * @param exceptionSupplier exception to throw if key is not available.
089     */
090    default void addSignedInput(Transaction tx, TransactionInputData in, Supplier<? extends RuntimeException> exceptionSupplier) {
091        tx.addSignedInput(in.toOutPoint(),
092                in.script(),
093                in.amount(),
094                keyForInput(in).orElseThrow(exceptionSupplier),
095                Transaction.SigHash.ALL,
096                false);
097    }
098
099    /**
100     * Return an ECKey, if available for the provided input
101     * @param input A transaction input
102     * @return The ECKey or empty
103     */
104    Optional<ECKey> keyForInput(TransactionInputData input);
105
106    default Optional<ECKey> pubKeyForInput(TransactionInputData input) {
107        return keyForInput(input).map(ECKey::fromPublicOnly);
108    }
109}
